name: Tianlong
description: The Tianlong, or Celestial Dragon, is a powerful and revered figure in Chinese mythology. Unlike some other dragons, the Tianlong is primarily associated with the heavens and is often depicted as guarding the celestial palaces and drawing the chariots of the gods. It represents the divine and embodies the power of the sky. The Tianlong is considered the most majestic of the Chinese dragons, symbolizing strength, wisdom, and benevolence. It is a symbol of the Emperor and imperial authority, embodying the mandate of heaven. As a bringer of rain and favorable weather, the Tianlong ensures agricultural prosperity and the well-being of the land. Its presence is a sign of good fortune and divine favor, making it a central figure in Chinese art, literature, and folklore. Its role is more passive than some other dragons, acting as a guardian and a symbol of cosmic order rather than an active participant in earthly conflicts. Its scales shimmer with celestial light, and its roar echoes through the heavens, commanding respect and awe.
subtitle: Celestial Dragon of Chinese Mythology
appearance: The Tianlong typically appears as a serpentine dragon with a long, scaled body, four legs with sharp claws, and a majestic head adorned with antlers or horns. Its scales are often depicted as being golden or azure, shimmering with celestial light. It may also have a pearl under its chin, a symbol of wisdom and spiritual essence. The Tianlong is often shown amidst clouds, signifying its connection to the heavens.
habitat: Celestial palaces, the heavens, mountaintops (as a temporary abode)
behavior: The Tianlong is generally depicted as benevolent and wise. It is a guardian of the celestial realm and a symbol of imperial authority. It ensures the balance of nature and brings favorable weather for agriculture. While powerful, it is not typically aggressive unless provoked or when defending its charge.
weaknesses: Corruption of the Mandate of Heaven

keyMyths.title: The Tianlong and the Drought
keyMyths.description: During a severe drought, the Emperor prayed to the Tianlong for rain. The Tianlong, moved by the suffering of the people, descended from the heavens and brought forth a life-giving storm that ended the drought and restored prosperity to the land. This solidified the Tianlong's reputation as a benevolent protector.

keyMyths.title: The Tianlong's Pearl
keyMyths.description: A legend tells of a fisherman who found a luminous pearl. Unbeknownst to him, this pearl was a treasure of the Tianlong. When the Tianlong appeared to reclaim its pearl, the fisherman, instead of resisting, respectfully returned it. Impressed by his integrity, the Tianlong granted the fisherman good fortune for the rest of his days.

keyMyths.title: The Celestial Chariot
keyMyths.description: The Tianlong are often depicted pulling the chariots of deities across the sky, most notably those of the Jade Emperor. These dragons ensure the smooth and timely passage of the gods, demonstrating their role in maintaining the order of the cosmos. Their flight is said to bring auspicious omens.

sources.author: Williams, C.A.S.
sources.description: A comprehensive guide to the symbolism inherent in Chinese art, including detailed entries on dragons and their various forms.
sources.text: A comprehensive guide to the symbolism inherent in Chinese art, including detailed entries on dragons and their various forms.

sources.author: Visser, Marinus Willem de
sources.work: The Dragon in China and Japan
sources.description: A thorough examination of the dragon's role in Chinese and Japanese culture, covering its mythological origins, symbolism, and artistic representations.
sources.text: A thorough examination of the dragon's role in Chinese and Japanese culture, covering its mythological origins, symbolism, and artistic representations.

sources.author: Eberhard, Wolfram
sources.work: A Dictionary of Chinese Symbols: Hidden Symbols in Chinese Life and Thought
sources.description: A valuable resource for understanding the meaning behind various Chinese symbols, including the dragon and its association with power, prosperity, and good fortune.
sources.text: A valuable resource for understanding the meaning behind various Chinese symbols, including the dragon and its association with power, prosperity, and good fortune.
